business.headline: NPS, EPF or mutual funds: Where should you invest Rs 5,000 for 10 yearsYou set aside Rs 5,000 every month with one simple goal, i.e., to build something meaningful over 10 years. But the real question is where to invest it. NPS, EPF or mutual funds? Let's have a closer look.

Sensex slips 123 points, Nifty ends below 24,200 as markets erase early gainsThe BSE Sensex closed at 77,988.68, down 122.56 points or 0.16%. The NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,196.75, lower by 34.55 points or 0.14%.

Sensex falls 1,000 points after early gains: What should investors do?The BSE Sensex moved within a wide range of 1,055.39 points, touching a high of 78,730.32 and a low of 77,674.93. The NSE Nifty 50 also saw similar movement, trading in a range of 298.15 points between a high of 24,400.95 and a low of 24,102.80.

Income below exemption limit? These 3 transactions make ITR filing compulsoryNo taxable income doesn't always mean no ITR. Certain transactions can bring you under the tax net, even if your earnings are below the basic limit.

From Rs 50 to Rs 200: How you can save up to Rs 1.05 lakh with meal vouchersWhat if your daily lunch could help you save tax? With the limit rising from Rs 50 to Rs 200 per meal, meal vouchers are suddenly becoming a powerful tax-saving tool for salaried employees.

DA hike has been delayed, but will you get it this year?If you've been waiting for the DA hike announcement, you might have noticed the silence this time. It hasn't come when expected, but does that mean trouble, or just a delay?

Workforce shrinks, pay hikes normalise: How TCS is reshaping hiring amid AI boomTCS has restored its salary cycle, but with more selective hiring and differentiated pay, signalling a shift towards skills and performance as the key drivers of workforce growth.

Mark Mobius dies at 89: The investor who took global money to emerging marketsMark Mobius built his reputation by investing in emerging markets that many investors once avoided due to political risk, weak governance and currency volatility. His approach helped draw billions of dollars into these regions.

8th Pay Commission: Will Rs 69,000 minimum pay actually happen?The proposal, submitted by the staff side of the National Council–Joint Consultative Machinery, has quickly grabbed attention for the scale of the increase it seeks.
Sensex opens 400 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; IT stocks upThe S&P BSE Sensex added 550.07 points to 78,661.31, while the NSE Nifty50 was up by 158.10 points to 24,389.40 as of 9:27 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market continue its rally or fall today?Early indicators suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,317.5 at 7:59 am, pointing to an opening above Wednesday's close of 24,231.3 for the Nifty 50.

TCS filing made easier: Income Tax Dept brings in new Form 143, replacing 27EQThe move is part of a wider effort to simplify tax processes under the updated income-tax framework and reduce errors in filing.

Indian delegation to visit US from April 20 as trade talks resumeAn Indian delegation led by the chief negotiator will visit the US from April 20. This will be the first in-person meeting between the two sides in about three to four months.
Sensex ends 1,264 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; IT stocks jumpThe S&P BSE Sensex jumped 1,263.67 points to close at 78,111.24, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 38.65 points to end at 24,231.30.

Is India's gold loan boom quietly building a default risk?Gold loans have grown nearly 3.8 times since March 2022. Their share in the retail credit market has also increased sharply, rising from 5.9% to 11.1% by December 2025. This makes gold loans the second-largest retail loan category after home loans.

DA hike update: Central government staff to protest on April 16Weeks after it was expected, the DA hike for January 2026 is still pending. With no update from the government, central government employee body is preparing to protest on April 16.

Zero tax to pay? 3 big reasons why you should still file your ITRIf your income is low and your taxes come to zero, you might think filing an ITR is not needed. But skipping it could mean missing out on some useful benefits that go beyond just paying tax.

8th Pay Commission: Unions push for Rs 69,000 minimum pay and 6% annual hikeA significant rise in minimum basic pay to Rs 69,000 is at the heart of the demands. The committee has suggested a fitment factor of 3.83, which would substantially raise existing salaries and pensions.

Don't let these 5 mistakes weaken your term insurance coverA term insurance plan can be your family's strongest safety net, but only if it's chosen wisely. Knowing the common mistakes beforehand can help you choose a policy that truly protects your loved ones.

I lost Rs 10,000 in a week as war hit stock markets. Why I didn't stop my SIPsAs the West Asia war sent markets into free fall, I lost Rs 10,000 in a week but didn't stop my SIPs, even when everything on screen told me to. Here's what happened next.

entertainment.headline: Udta Teer: Ayushmann Khurrana, Sara Ali Khan's spy comedy gets a release date Ayushmann Khurrana and Sara Ali Khan are teaming up for the spy comedy Udta Teer. The film, directed by Akash A Kaushik, has locked in a release date.

Jana Nayagan leak: 3 key accused arrested by Cyber Crime Wing The Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested three key suspects involved in the online leak of Jana Nayagan. This move comes amid heightened efforts to curb illegal distribution and protect the film industry from such breaches.

Exclusive: No imitating Michael, say Jaafar Jackson and director Antoine Fuqua Actor Jaafar Jackson and director Antoine Fuqua have said that the upcoming musical, Michael, does not intend to imitate Michael Jackson.

Kangana Ranaut supports 'top-most actress' Deepika Padukone's 8-hour work demand Kangana Ranaut weighed in on the debate sparked by Deepika Padukone's reported stance on an eight-hour workday, backing her right to set boundaries at this stage in her career.

Jana Nayagan leak row: Madras High Court bans illegal streaming, cable broadcast The Madras High Court has issued a temporary ban on unlawful streaming of Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan. This move aims to safeguard the big-budget project from further financial losses caused by illegal online distribution.

Yash likens Toxic to Oscar-winning film Sinners. Check out the parallel he draws Kannada star Yash awaits the release of his next film Toxic. In an interview, he called the premise unique and layered, likening it to the Oscar-winning Hollywood film Sinners.

A drop of divinity: Mahesh Bhatt says granddaughter Raha made him rediscover life Mahesh Bhatt opened up about the profound joy of being a grandfather, calling granddaughter Raha Kapoor "a drop of divinity" in his life.

Puneet Superstar to Deepak Kalal: Samay Raina's wild OTT plans that never happened Comedian Samay Raina recently revealed that he had ambitious plans to launch a creator-driven OTT platform featuring unique shows with Puneet Superstar and Deepak Kalal, but these were halted due to the India's Got Latent controversy.

Actor SJ Suryah reveals reasons for rejecting Lubber Pandhu role At the LIK success meet, actor SJ Suryah shared reasons behind declining the lead role in Lubber Pandhu, which later became a blockbuster. He spoke about trusting his instincts and praised the director's unique storytelling style.
Ek Din new trailer: Junaid Khan and Sai Pallavi find hope in magical romance The new trailer of Sai Pallavi and Junaid Khan's Ek Din promises a unique love story filled with hope and magic.

Taylor Swift wedding dress inspired by Elizabeth Taylor's iconic 1950 gown? Taylor Swift is turning to Elizabeth Taylor for wedding dress inspiration. She is said to be embracing vintage glamour inspired by the Hollywood icon's first bridal look. The singer's wedding with Travis Kelce is set for July 3 in New York.

Vignesh Shivan breaks silence on 'normal people' viral video controversy Director Vignesh Shivan addressed the backlash over 'normal people' comment made by an organiser during an event. He explained the video was edited to misrepresent his words.

From Hollywood to OnlyFans: American Pie actor Shannon Elizabeth explains move Actor Shannon Elizabeth shared that she is joining OnlyFans to take control of her career and be closer to her fans. She expressed her desire to show a new, more confident side of herself after years in Hollywood.

KKK 15: Farrhana, Harsh Gujral confirmed; Gaurav, Samarth in talks | Exclusive Farrhana Bhatt and Harsh Gujral have been confirmed as participants in Khatron Ke Khiladi 15. Bigg Boss 19 winner Gaurav Khanna and Laughter Chefs contestant Samarth Jurel are also in talks to participate in the reality show.

Vignesh Shivan reacts to piracy of Vijay's Jana Nayagan, supports film's editor Director Vignesh Shivan expressed deep disappointment over the piracy of Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan, calling it a crisis for the film industry. He defended his editor and urged fans to watch the film only through legal channels to support the makers.

Yash reacts to Ramayana's VFX amid criticism, calls it work-in-progress Yash addressed the chatter around Ramayana: Part I, calling its VFX a work in progress while promising a "top-notch" final product.

How Salman Khan inspired Dhurandhar actor Bimal Oberoi's song Firdaus Dhurandhar actor Bimal Oberoi shared how Salman Khan's immense popularity and the fervent devotion of his fans in Vienna inspired him to create the track Firdaus.

Scenes cut, casting surprises: Mrunal Thakur says Son of Sardaar 2 left her shaken Mrunal Thakur opened up about feeling blindsided while working on Son of Sardaar 2, hinting at creative decisions that didn't sit right with her. From unexpected casting choices to key scenes being cut, the actor reflected on a shaken sense of trust.

They were drunk: Ex-club manager on Katy Perry-Ruby Rose sexual assault case Former Australian nightclub manager commented on actor Ruby Rose's allegations against singer Katy Perry. He said both were drunk and described the decade-old event as a security nightmare with around 600 people present.

general news.headline: SpiceJet plane hits Akasa aircraft while taxiing at Delhi airport, wing damagedA taxiing mishap at Delhi airport leaves two aircraft damaged and raises fresh questions over ground safety, as SpiceJet grounds its plane and details emerge on how the incident unfolded.

North to South, no bias: PM Modi's guarantee on delimitation processPrime Minister Narendra Modi addressed a special Parliament session on Thursday, assuring that the delimitation process will not be biased or unfair. His remarks referenced concerns and fierce objections by Opposition MPs, who have raised that the process would significantly impact non-BJP states, especially those in the South.

No man or state will be hit: Law Minister on women's reservationLaw Minister Arjun Meghwal said the main objective of the bill is to give women their due rights. The government has come up with a simple formula to ensure no man or state suffers from the implementation of the quota, he assured.

Vinod Kambli's health worsens: Tendulkar joins WhatsApp group raising supportVinod Kambli continues to fight serious health challenges, with memory issues still a concern. As support grows, Sachin Tendulkar and others have stepped in to help through a private initiative.

No bindi, tilak, but hijab allowed? Lenskart clarifies after backlash over dress codeLenskart faced backlash over its grooming guide as people highlighted that the eyewear startup allows hijab but prohibits bindi, tilak and kalawa. Lenskart founder and CEO Peyush Bansal clarified that the company's policies had evolved, and the document didn't represent its "present guidelines".

TCS Nashik case reaches SC, plea seeks directions to control forced conversionsThe TCS Nashik conversion case has reached the Supreme Court, with a petition alleging that the incident has "shaken the conscience of the nation" and seeking directions from the Centre and states to take stringent measures to curb forced conversions.

Send your wife to me if you want a child: TCS Nashik employee's explosive testimonyNarrating his ordeal to India Today, the man alleged that his team leaders made him offer namaz, recite the kalma, and wear a skullcap. The harassment, he said, crossed all limits when one of the accused mocked his personal life, suggesting he "send his wife" if he wanted a child despite medical treatment.

Is the timing of delimitation bill BJP's gamble for Bengal polls?The BJP-led Centre's push to add extra seats in Parliament is pivoted on reserving 33% of the seats for women. But the timing of the special session of Parliament for introducing the bills coincides with elections in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu. Is it the BJP's bid to raid the Trinamool's Lakshmir Bhandar of women voters?

Don't care for job: House helps, workers rush to Bengal to vote amid citizenship scareThousands of migrant workers from West Bengal across India's metro cities and industrial hubs are rushing home to vote, even risking jobs and wages. Due to the SIR exercise and political messaging, a fear has gripped the Bengali migrants that they might lose their voting rights, and even citizenship, if they don't vote this time.

These things happen: TCS Nashik BPO HR told woman not to report harassmentExplaining the role of the two female accused, the Nashik Police Commissioner said that the operations and HR head of the unit had allegedly discouraged a survivor from going ahead with the complaint, saying "these things happen" while siding with the accused.

59 for Tamil Nadu, 30 for Kerala: BJP MP says delimitation move best deal for SouthThe key concern for the South over the Delimitation Bill is that it would cost them seats and influence. However, BJP MP Tejasvi Surya asserted that the government would increase the share of all states uniformly by 50%, which would, in fact, benefit the southern states.

Jana Nayagan leak: Freelance editor stole footage from studio, 3 arrestedThe Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested three key suspects involved in the online leak of Jana Nayagan. This move comes amid heightened efforts to curb illegal distribution and protect the film industry from such breaches.

Truth about Pakistan, Munir revealed: How Iran leader visit video spills beansPakistan army chief Asim Munir's Tehran visit has once again revealed who holds power in Pakistan. While Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi stood back, Munir was the first person to de-board the flight in Tehran and was greeted by Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi.

Give all your tickets to Muslim women: Amit Shah jabs Akhilesh Yadav over quotaA sharp exchange took place between Union Home Minister Amit Shah and Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav over reservation, Muslim representation, and caste census during the debate on the Women Reservation-linked delimitation bill in Lok Sabha on Thursday.

Did an Indian report scuttle a secret US-Indonesia airspace deal?A secret deal between the US and Indonesia, giving American jets sweeping access to Indonesian airspace, failed to go through at the last moment after the plan was leaked by an Indian media outlet. It led to a massive backlash in Indonesia, prompting the government to put it on the back burner.

Live: PM Modi offers 'blank cheque' to oppn to claim credit for women quota billSpecial Parliament Session Live Updates: The three-day sitting from April 16–18 is underway with the government tabling the Women's Reservation Bill alongside the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, the Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill and the Delimitation Bill in the Lok Sabha, where Union Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal opened the debate. With the NDA holding 292 seats against the Opposition's 233, the two-thirds majority requirement has put the numbers battle at the centre of the proceedings. Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday urged the opposition not to politicise the government's push to implement the women's reservation law, warning that parties opposing it in the past had paid a price in elections. Speaking in the Lok Sabha, he assured that the proposed delimitation exercise would not disadvantage any state across the country. Modi appealed to MPs to back the legislation in national interest, calling it a crucial opportunity to bring women into policymaking. The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, along with the Delimitation Bill and a measure for Union territories, was introduced after a vote, with 251 members in favour and 185 against. The row has also spilled onto the streets, with protests led by Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin, even as the INDIA bloc continues to protest inside the Parliament. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.

Kolkata man expresses 7 emotions through eyes in Hum Tere Pyaar Mein lip-syncIn a short video that has gone viral on social media, Kolkata-based Bibhu lip-synced to a cover of the iconic song Hum Tere Pyaar Mein Saara Aalam, originally immortalised by Lata Mangeshkar.

Live: Netanyahu to speak to Lebanon President, Beirut wants ceasefire before talksUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Tensions in the Middle East remain on edge as diplomacy and military pressure move in parallel, with Pakistan once again stepping in as a mediator. Pakistan's Army Chief Asim Munir has arrived in Tehran carrying messages between Washington and Tehran after talks in Islamabad failed to yield a breakthrough, even as Iranian officials confirmed that "several messages have been exchanged through Pakistan" since the last round. The United States, meanwhile, has tightened its naval blockade on Iranian ports, claiming it has "completely halted economic trade going into and out of Iran by sea", prompting a sharp warning from Tehran that it could block shipping routes across the Red Sea, the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman if pressure continues. At the same time, US President Donald Trump has struck an optimistic tone, saying the war is "close to over" and hinting that fresh talks could take place in Pakistan in the coming days, though no timeline has been confirmed. The fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, set to expire soon, adds urgency to the situation, even as parallel tensions persist elsewhere in the region, including ongoing exchanges between Israel and Hezbollah and discussions around a possible Lebanon truce. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

The Rs 1 crore mistake? Why foreign degree returns aren't guaranteed anymoreMany Indian students invest lakhs in overseas education expecting quick returns. Experts say that assumption is flawed. From job uncertainty to hidden costs, the real return on investment depends on multiple factors. This story breaks down the biggest mistakes students make while calculating ROI and what they often overlook today.

1 vote for me, 1 lakh for Ambani? Tejasvi Surya slams Opposition on delimitationFraming delimitation as a constitutional mandate, not a political choice, Surya cited Articles 81 and 82, which require balancing the population-to-seat ratio and redrawing constituencies.

Costliest drone, flying radar, F-35 destroyed: Iran war is bleeding US airpowerWhile American fighter jets dominated the skies over Iran, reports are increasingly emerging of the huge losses that the US suffered in terms of its airpower. From the costly Triton drone, MQ9B drones to F-35 jets and an AWACS, the losses the US suffered in the Iran war run into over $720 million.

It's not a gift, it's her right: 10 top PM quotes on women's reservation billPrime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday mocked Opposition parties over women's reservation, saying they were free to take full credit for the legislation if it helped ensure its progress.

'Still sleeping?' The most annoying question in Indian homesTell us you've never been judged for sleeping a little extra or taking an additional nap, and we'll stop right here. The truth is, Indians love shaming others for sleep, even though it is one of the most essential things the body needs to function properly.

Women haven't forgiven those who opposed women's quota: PM cautions OppositionStating that the proposed 33 per cent reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and state assemblies was "her right, not a gift", PM Modi called on all parties to support the legislation and avoid giving it a political colour.

sports.headline: Lalit Modi explains why IND vs PAK bilaterals may never happen Lalit Modi says political tensions and a "politicised" PCB are key reasons behind the absence of India vs Pakistan bilaterals, adding he does not see a revival happening anytime soon.

MS Dhoni back? CSK great sets off to Hyderabad, raises comeback hopes IPL 2026: After opting not to travel to Guwahati and Bengaluru for their previous away games, MS Dhoni joined the Chennai Super KIngs squad which left for Hyderabad on Thursday. CSK will play SunRisers Hyderabad on Saturday, April 18.

RCB's golden era? Bangar predicts title spree for Royal Challengers Bengaluru Sanjay Bangar sees RCB entering a golden era after their maiden IPL title, backing the in-form side to build a winning culture and challenge for multiple championships in the coming years.

Messi in legal trouble over missed Argentina friendly Lionel Messi is facing legal trouble over a missed Argentina friendly in the US. A Miami-based promoter claims his absence led to major financial losses under a multi-million dollar deal.

Vinod Kambli's health worsens: Tendulkar joins WhatsApp group raising support Vinod Kambli continues to fight serious health challenges, with memory issues still a concern. As support grows, Sachin Tendulkar and others have stepped in to help through a private initiative.

IPL: MI rope in Punjab all-rounder Krish Bhagat to replace Atharva Ankolekar Mumbai Indians sign Krish Bhagat as replacement for injured Atharva Ankolekar in IPL 2026, with the Punjab all-rounder joining at base price as MI look to revive a slow start.

Vaishali's rise to Candidates champion earns Judith Polgar's praise R Vaishali's stunning run to Women's Candidates title draws praise from Judit and Susan Polgar on X, as the lowest-rated player seals a World Championship spot with a final-round win.

CSK's Khaleel Ahmed ruled out of IPL 2026 due to injury IPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have been dealt a big blow for their ongoing season with their pacer, Khaleel Ahmed being ruled out due to injury.

Rasikh always had it: RCB teammate credits pacer's experience for LSG heroics IPL 2026: Rasikh Salam's four-wicket haul against LSG drew praise, but not surprise, from Jitesh Sharma. The RCB keeper insisted the pacer always had the skillset, with experience and guidance now helping him deliver consistently.

Rohit Sharma fitness doubt adds to MI's worries against rampant PBKS IPL 2026, MI vs PBKS: Mumbai Indians have struggled so far, losing three matches on the trot, and face a tough challenge against the unbeaten Punjab Kings on Thursday at the Wankhede Stadium. Rohit Sharma's injury concern and Jasprit Bumrah's indifferent form have only added to their worries.

CSK writes to BCCI over 'Dosa Idly' song jibe at Chinnaswamy during RCB clash CSK write to BCCI over 'Dosa Idly' song and DJ remarks at Chinnaswamy during IPL 2026 clash vs RCB, raising concerns over match-day conduct and rivalry tensions.

Vaishali's Candidates triumph: A win vs odds that redefines the underdog termLowest-rated in the field, counted out before it began, Vaishali still found a way. This wasn't just a win, it was a grind through chaos, pressure and moments that could have ended it all.

Pat Cummins cleared for IPL 2026 return: Will he take part in SRH vs CSK?IPL 2026: Pat Cummins has been cleared to return after recovering from his back injury. However, he is unlikely to feature in SRH's clash against CSK as he targets a later comeback.

Frustrated Real Madrid slam referee over soft Camavinga red vs BayernReal Madrid's UCL exit was overshadowed by controversy over Camavinga's red card. The decision proved decisive as Bayern capitalised late to seal progression.

IPL Play Of The Day: Rasikh Salam beats Mukul Choudhary in duel, earns RCB trustIPL 2026: Rasikh Salam came into his own for RCB and defeated the big-hitting Mukul Choudhary in a last-over Mexican standoff. With RCB finally finding their third seamer, Rasikh could be the difference in their run towards a second consecutive title.

R Vaishali's Checkmate: From 'Pragg's sister' to World Championship challengerLong the other Rameshbabu in a house of genius, the elder sibling has finally pulled rank. By conquering the Candidates in Cyprus, R Vaishali secured a World Championship date with China's Ju Wenjun, and a name of her own.

KKR should open with Green: Aaron Finch on all-rounder's struggles in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Cameron Green has struggled since returning from injury and being used out of position, with poor performances in both batting and bowling adding to KKR's concerns.

Not as versatile as Bumrah, but as effective: Behind Josh Hazlewood's IPL successRoyal Challengers Bengaluru fast bowler Josh Hazlewood admitted he does not possess the same range of variations as Jasprit Bumrah, but said his success in the Indian Premier League has come from sticking to accuracy and a simple method.

Virat Kohli embraces modern T20 approach, but keeps focus on smart cricketVirat Kohli said he is constantly looking to raise the bar when it comes to adapting to the modern ways of T20 cricket. However, the RCB star stressed that smart cricket is also needed after the win over LSG.

Bittersweet moment: Watch Pragg celebrate sister Vaishali's historic Candidates winAn emotional R Vaishali celebrated with her family members after winning the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament, sealing a historic title and a place in the World Championship. For R Praggnanandhaa, the celebrations carried a bittersweet note after his disappointing campaign in the tournament.

IPL 2026: Ruthless RCB blow away hapless LSG to top the tableRoyal Challengers Bengaluru climbed to the top of the table with a commanding win, handling a tricky home surface with assurance to outplay Lucknow Super Giants. Chasing a modest target, RCB wrapped up a five-wicket victory with 29 balls to spare, capping off a dominant all-round performance.

Carlos Alcaraz pulls out of Barcelona Open, days after losing No.1 rankingCarlos Alcaraz has withdrawn from the Barcelona Open due to a wrist injury, ending his chances of reclaiming the world No.1 ranking. His exit comes days after losing the top spot to Jannik Sinner, who beat him in the Monte Carlo Masters final.

R Vaishali becomes first Indian woman to win Candidates Chess, to fight for world titleR Vaishali clinched the 2026 FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament on Wednesday, earning the right to challenge China's reigning champion Ju Wenjun for the Women's World Chess Championship crown later this year.

No late heroics: Rishabh Pant's brave return from injury ends in disappointmentIPL 2026, RCB vs LSG: Rishabh Pant showed grit by returning to bat after an elbow injury but failed to make an impact, managing just one run in the match between Lucknow Super Giants and Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

IPL 2026: CSK face MS Dhoni dilemma as return raises impact sub questionMS Dhoni's likely return has presented Chennai Super Kings with a selection dilemma just as the side has begun to build momentum in IPL 2026. Former Australia captain Aaron Finch has suggested that Dhoni could be utilised in a more defined, situation-based role, including as an Impact Player, amid concerns over his recent struggles with batting position and finishing games.

Rohit ruled out? MI to wait until PBKS matchday to take call on availabilityMumbai Indians are set to wait until match day to make a call about Rohit Sharma's availability for the clash against Punjab Kings on Thursday, April 16. Rohit suffered the injury during the loss to RCB.

IPL 2026, RCB vs LSG: Kohli overcomes injury scare, but named Impact playerVirat Kohli has shaken off an injury concern to be ready to play the game against Lucknow Super Giants at the Chinnaswamy stadium on Wednesday, April 15. However, Kohli has been named as the impact substitute for the game.

Can anyone say no to Virat, Rohit? Munaf backs Gautam Gambhir's hardline styleMunaf Patel said Gautam Gambhir's willingness to say no to icons like Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma has helped maintain order during a tricky transition period, warning that Indian cricket would collapse without his authority.

Ben Stokes lucky to be alive after nasty facial injury: Thankfully, still hereBen Stokes said that he is happy to be alive after suffering a horrific facial injury in February while training with his county side Durham. Stokes was hit on the face by a stray ball and had to undergo surgery to repair the injury.

Hugo Ekitike's World Cup hopes hit after injury in Liverpool lossHugo Ekitike is set for a lengthy spell on the sidelines after suffering a suspected Achilles tendon rupture during Liverpool's defeat to Paris Saint-Germain. The injury comes as a major blow to both Liverpool's season and France's World Cup hopes, especially given his impressive return of 19 goals this campaign.

Josh Inglis for Nicholas Pooran? Irfan Pathan flags key concern for LSGIPL 2026: Irfan Pathan has said Nicholas Pooran may be under pressure at Lucknow Super Giants after a poor IPL run and could be replaced by Josh Inglis, when the Australian joins the camp. Pooran has scored just 41 runs in four innings at a strike rate of 85.41.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
